channel.title: GNS3 :: SE How-To Build Lab
channel.description: Verify Nested Virtualization is Enabled ​ From PVE Shell cat /sys/module/kvm_intel/parameters/nested If it returns “Y”, proceed to GNS3 VM Creation step If it returns “N”, follow steps in URL below to enable https://pve.proxmox.com/wiki/Nested_Virtualization https://devopstales.github.io/virtualization/install-vmware-in-proxmox/ Create GNS3 VM If you get the error No route to host with any of the following steps, use the Troubleshoot Ansible steps ​ RDP to OOB from Terminal in OOB cd /home/fortinet/automation/ansible/ubuntu ./create_ubuntu_vm.sh ../vars/all-hosts.yml <PVE server name> gns3 ./start_stop_remove_vm.sh ../vars/all-hosts.yml <PVE server name> gns3 started Configure GNS3 VM ​ RDP to OOB Make sure GNS3 VM has fully started (GUI is up and running) before exeucting the following. Suggest opening GNS3’s console window on PVE to verify before continuing. ./wallpaper_update.sh gns3 ./configure_gns3.sh gns3 ​ RDP to GNS3 From the GNS3 VM’s terminal execute the script below When prompted whether non-root users should be allowed to use wireshark and ubridge, select ‘Yes’ both times cd /home/fortinet/Downloads GNS3_Install.sh VM will reboot when finished Install GNS3 GUI on Laptop ​ Login and Download GNS3 Login to GNS3 https://www.gns3.com/account/login Download GNS3 https://www.gns3.com/software/download ​ Options During Install Uncheck all options but MSVC Runtime 2017 and GNS3 Desktop “No” to Solarwinds Engineer’s Toolset ​ Configure Latop Software Select “Edit/Preferences” Select “Server”/“Main server”
